message:
fix(cli,server): propagate database-busy errors instead of silently swallowing them

Previously, when SQLite doctor/health probes encountered a locked database
(another Agent Mail process or doctor command holding the WAL), the error
was either silently swallowed (returning Ok(false) or Ok(true) depending
on a lock_fallback flag) or conflated with corruption. This made it
impossible for users to distinguish "database is genuinely corrupt" from
"another process is using it right now."

CLI changes (lib.rs):
- Add sqlite_doctor_busy_error() helper that produces a clear, actionable
  error message naming the busy database path and suggesting the user
  stop competing processes
- Reorder error classification in handle_sqlite_compatibility_probe_result
  to check is_lock_error before is_sqlite_recovery_error, ensuring busy
  databases are never misclassified as corrupt
- Remove the lock_fallback parameter entirely — busy is always an error
  now, not a conditional pass/fail
- Add lock-error checks in the absolute-fallback and sanity-probe paths
- Add test: sqlite_doctor_sanity_reports_busy_database_as_resource_busy

Server startup probe changes (startup_checks.rs):
- Add lock-error detection to probe_integrity so a busy database during
  startup is reported as a clear probe failure with remediation advice
  ("Stop any running am, mcp-agent-mail, or am doctor process")
- Expand executable_name_has_agent_mail_signature to recognize the short
  "am" binary name and "agent-mail"/"agent_mail" variants so process
  detection correctly identifies all Agent Mail executables
- Refactor run_http_startup_preflight_probes to detect when an existing
  Agent Mail listener is on the configured port and defer db-lock and
  integrity probes until after port handoff, preventing false failures
  during zero-downtime restarts
- Move daemon start goroutine launch after the DaemonStateStarting store
  write so immediate status checks don't race the startup
- Add port-matching validation in startMemoryDaemonAsync to prevent
  adopting a foreign cm daemon running on a different port
- Add cloneMemoryDaemonInfo for defensive copy semantics in the store
- Add currentMemoryDaemonInfo state machine that respects Starting/Stopping
  transitional states instead of always re-probing
- Update and add tests for all new behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
